channel.title: YouTube on yexca'Blog
channel.description: Recent content in YouTube on yexca'Blog

item.title: Downloading YouTube Videos on Linux
item.description: 📢 This article was translated by gemini-3-flash-preview Introduction Since we’ve already covered the Windows command line, we can’t skip Linux. Windows article: Downloading YouTube videos via Command Line Download yt-dlp I recommend setting up a Python environment first, then heading to Releases · yt-dlp/yt-dlp · GitHub to download yt-dlp. If you don’t want to deal with Python, just download the yt-dlp_linux binary. After downloading, grant it execution permissions and move …

item.title: Embedding External Video Links in Blogs
item.description: 📢 This article was translated by gemini-3-flash-preview This article was written by Hiyoung Original article: https://blog.hiyoung.icu/2022/08/11/607cac6fe835/ Most mainstream streaming platforms support embedding code for external links. However, as of August 11, 2022, Bilibili’s (when is it shutting down?) default embed link defaults to 360P and doesn’t allow quality adjustments. It’s pretty frustrating compared to YouTube. Below are the parameters and methods for …

item.title: Download YouTube Videos from the Command Line
item.description: 📢 This article was translated by gemini-2.5-flash Intro What dev doesn’t want to run stuff from the command line just to look cool? I knew about youtube-dl before but thought it looked too complicated to bother with. Tried it today and it’s actually super slick! Setup Install Python 3.7+ Just head to the Python official site and install it. Make sure to check Add Python x.x to PATH during installation.
